name: TM01 Work Up
description: Raises the user's Attack and Special Attack by one stage each. A versatile setup move useful for mixed attackers.
method: Given by Captain Ilima after meeting him on Route 2

name: TM10 Hidden Power
description: A special move whose type depends on the user's IVs. Can be any type except Normal and Fairy.
method: Given by an NPC inside the Hau'oli City mall

name: TM31 Brick Break
description: A Fighting-type physical move with 75 power that also breaks Light Screen and Reflect barriers.
method: Found deep inside Verdant Cavern, accessible after completing the trial

name: TM46 Thief
description: A Dark-type physical move with 60 power that steals the target's held item if the user isn't holding one.
method: Found in the back room of Verdant Cavern after completing the trial

name: Normalium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Normal-type moves into the Z-Move Breakneck Blitz. The power depends on the base move used.
method: Reward for completing Captain Ilima's trial and defeating the Totem Pokemon

name: Fightinium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Fighting-type moves into the Z-Move All-Out Pummeling.
method: Reward for defeating Island Kahuna Hala in the Melemele Grand Trial

name: Waterium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Water-type moves into the Z-Move Hydro Vortex.
method: Reward for completing Captain Lana's trial and defeating Totem Araquanid

name: Firium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Fire-type moves into the Z-Move Inferno Overdrive.
method: Reward for completing Captain Kiawe's trial and defeating Totem Alolan Marowak

name: Grassium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Grass-type moves into the Z-Move Bloom Doom.
method: Reward for completing Captain Mallow's trial and defeating Totem Lurantis

name: Fire Stone
description: A fire-patterned stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Growlithe into Arcanine, Eevee into Flareon, and Vulpix into Ninetales.
method: Found in Diglett's Tunnel. Also available at Konikoni City stone shop for 3000 Poke Dollars.

name: Thunder Stone
description: A thunderbolt-patterned stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Pikachu into Alolan Raichu (in Alola), Eevee into Jolteon, and Charjabug into Vikavolt (in some games).
method: Found on Route 9. Also available at Konikoni City stone shop for 3000 Poke Dollars.

name: Water Stone
description: A water-drop shaped stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Eevee into Vaporeon, Poliwhirl into Poliwrath, Shellder into Cloyster, and others.
method: Hidden item in the tide pool area on Route 8. Also available at Konikoni City stone shop for 3000 Poke Dollars.

name: Moon Stone
description: A moon-shaped stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Clefairy into Clefable, Jigglypuff into Wigglytuff, Nidorina into Nidoqueen, Nidorino into Nidoking, and others.
method: Can be found via Poke Pelago Isle Aphun mining. Also held by wild Clefairy (5% chance) or Lunatone.

name: Dusk Stone
description: A sinister dark stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Murkrow into Honchkrow, Misdreavus into Mismagius, Lampent into Chandelure, and Doublade into Aegislash.
method: Found on Poni Wilds. Also obtainable from Poke Pelago Isle Aphun mining.

name: Z-Ring
description: A bracelet set with Z-Crystals that enables you to use Z-Moves in battle. Crafted by Kahuna Hala from the Sparkling Stone given to you by Tapu Koko. Later upgraded to Z-Power Ring at Ultra Megalopolis.
method: Given by Island Kahuna Hala before the Melemele Grand Trial, crafted from your Sparkling Stone

name: Ride Pager
description: A device that lets you summon Ride Pokemon to help traverse the overworld. Replaces HMs from previous games. Ride Pokemon include Tauros (rock smash), Stoutland (item finder), Lapras (surf), Charizard (fly), and more.
method: Received after defeating Island Kahuna Hala in the Melemele Grand Trial

ridePokemon.name: Tauros Charge
ridePokemon.function: Smash rocks and move quickly on land

ridePokemon.name: Sharpedo Jet
ridePokemon.function: Fast surfing and break rocks in water

name: Rotom Dex
description: A Pokedex inhabited by a Rotom, giving it a personality. It provides a map, Pokemon data, advice, and its Roto Loto feature gives you useful items and boosts. In Ultra Sun/Moon, it can also use Z-Rotom Power for a second Z-Move in battle.
method: Professor Kukui gives you the Pokedex, and a Rotom inhabits it after you choose your starter

name: Amulet Coin
description: A held item that doubles prize money from trainer battles when the holder participates. Essential for accumulating money throughout the game.
method: Talk to the gentleman inside the ranch house on Paniola Ranch
effect: Doubles prize money from trainer battles if the holder enters the battle

name: Lucky Egg
description: A held item that boosts EXP gained by 50% for the holder. Extremely valuable for leveling up Pokemon quickly.
method: Given by Professor Kukui in his lab on Route 1 after registering 50 Pokemon in the Pokedex
effect: Holder gains 50% more EXP from battles

name: Soothe Bell
description: A held item that increases the rate at which a Pokemon's friendship grows. Useful for Pokemon that evolve via friendship like Pichu, Eevee (Espeon/Umbreon), Zubat line, and Alolan Meowth.
method: Given by an NPC on Route 3 near the bridge
